frameworks.name: amplitude coupling of cortical oscillations
frameworks.one_line: transient coincidences of high-amplitude oscillatory events as inter-areal coupling

frameworks.name: phase coherence
frameworks.one_line: alternative oscillatory mechanism for inter-areal interactions

frameworks.name: large-scale functional brain networks
frameworks.one_line: dynamic emergence of distributed networks supporting cognition

core_question: Does inter-areal amplitude coupling of cortical oscillations support cognition through transient high-amplitude events rather than sustained coupling?
manipulations: comparison of correct vs other decision trials
key_findings: amplitude coupling reflects coincident high-amplitude events, not sustained coupling
parieto-frontal alpha/beta coincidences increase before correct decisions
visual, medial parietal, lateral prefrontal theta/alpha coincidences increase during attention allocation
interpretations: brain-wide orchestration of high-amplitude oscillatory events facilitates inter-areal interactions for efficient cognition
